symbols: swan wings — transformation and otherworldly nature, beings between animal and human realms
water — purification and the threshold between the mortal and supernatural worlds
reeds and marsh setting — the liminal space between land and water, the boundary of enchantment
description: Several female figures with large golden-feathered swan wings are gathered at the edge of a body of water among tall reeds. Some stand, some sit, and some wade in the blue-green water. Their wings are spread or folded in various positions. The central standing figure is draped in a light garment while others are partially nude. The color palette features golden yellows for the wings and feathers, greens for the reeds, and blue for the water. The style is decorative and illustrative.
